data.title: As an AI coding agent, I receive kudos for extracting helper functions and removing pass-through methods detected via structural analysis
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement sg-based structural kudos triggers T5 and T11 — function extraction to helper and pass-through method removal — using diff-to-sg-scope.sh with grep fallback when sg is absent.
**Why**: T5 and T11 recognize structural code quality improvements that require syntax-aware detection. They depend on diff-to-sg-scope.sh (Story B) providing a reliable sg interface with a formal output contract.
**Scope**:
- IN: T5 and T11 trigger implementations consuming diff-to-sg-scope.sh output; grep fallback implementations for both
- OUT: diff-to-sg-scope.sh itself (Story B); AST-based triggers (Story F)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, T5 awards kudos when diff-to-sg-scope.sh detects a function extracted to a helper in staged Python files (function definition moved from call site to separate helper function); grep fallback implements equivalent detection when sg is absent
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-T5
- When this story is complete, T11 awards kudos when diff-to-sg-scope.sh detects a pass-through method removed from staged Python files (method that only delegates to another method with no added logic); grep fallback implements equivalent detection when sg is absent
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-T11
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Include >= 3 shared test fixtures that verify both sg and grep code paths produce identical trigger/no-trigger decisions for T5 and T11. The grep fallback is only exercised on machines without sg; without parity tests it will diverge over time.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
